name: Dutch A2 Vocabulary
description: Elementary Dutch. Simple conversations about daily life. Shops, restaurants, introductions.

facts.explanation: aankomen — to arrive. Also: to depend, to gain weight, to grow, to be acquired
aanlopen — to encounter, meet. Also: to hasten, walk with speed, to walk toward, to continue, to endure, to frequent
aantal — countable amount. Also: a number, a plurality, a few
aanvallen — to attack.
aardappel — potato (tuber eaten as vegetable). Also: potato plant (Solanum tuberosum)
aarde — earth, soil, ground. Also: earth, ground
aarzelen — to hesitate.
acht — attention, heed.
adem — breath.
adres — address (direction for letters).
aflopen — to walk down. Also: to incline, to expire, to end, to come to an end
afscheid — farewell.
afspraak — arrangement, agreement, understanding. Also: appointment, date
afspreken — to agree, to make an agreement. Also: to make an appointment

agent — a police officer. Also: a police officer of the low(est) rank, constable, junior officer, an undercover agent; intelligence officer, secret agent, an agent (one who acts on behalf of another), a broker, a middleman, surrogate
al — already. Also: yet, emphatic modifier of adverbs, synonym of wel (“opposite of not”)
allemaal — Indicates that the speaker expects the listener to give a comprehensive list of examples.
alweer — once again, once more. Also: already, already again
anders — otherwise. Also: differently
angstig — fearful, anxious.
antwoord — answer, reply.
antwoorden — to answer, reply.
avond — evening, night.

baan — a road, way, path. Also: a track, lane, a job, professional occupation, orbit (path of one object around another), a job (task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode), court, field (place for playing sports or games, in particular non-team ball games)
baas — boss, chief, superior. Also: employer, manager, strong or tough guy, boss, master, expert (at something), whopper, large one (of its kind), fellow, boy, especially a youngling or novice
badkamer — bathroom.
bakken — to bake. Also: to pan-fry
bakker — baker (person who bakes and sells bread, etc).
bal — a ball or any object with such a shape. Also: testicle, nut, pass, shot, toff, posh person
balkon — balcony.
balzaal — ballroom.

band — bond, connection, relationship, liaison, tie (attachment, as in a relation). Also: band (all English senses, above, except for group of musicians), ribbon or object of similar shape, tire / tyre (e.g., a car tyre), tape (magnetic tape, video tape), belt (martial arts belt), belt (conveyor belt), interval relating to frequency or wavelength in electromagnetic phenomena, range of energy levels in a solid state material, interval in the light spectrum, bank (the bank of a pool table)
basisschool — elementary school (US), primary school (UK).
bedanken — to thank. Also: to decline, to reject [with voor] (with connotations of politeness)
bedelaar — a beggar, a mendicant, who requests alms or handouts. Also: a pauper, one who can't properly provide in his/her own/family's needs
bedenken — to bethink. Also: to think of, to make up, to reconsider, to change one's mind
bediende — servant, low-ranking employee (any gender). Also: employee, service industry worker (as opposed to manual labourers)
bedoelen — to intend, to have an aim. Also: to mean, to intend to communicate
bedriegen — to deceive. Also: to cheat on one's significant other
bedrijf — business, economic activity. Also: profession, company, firm, corporation, act, major unit in a theatre piece
bedroeven — to grieve, sadden.
been — leg, limb of a person, horse (other animals would have poten) and certain objects (again many have poten). Also: side, leg, the upper part of a sock, above the ankle
beer — boar (male swine). Also: buttress; protective external construction, notably against ice or supporting the weight of the main building, a boar-shaped type of battering ram, a male badger
beest — an animal, a beast. Also: an animal kept as livestock, a head, a cruel, wild, uncivilised, uninhibited or brutal person, jerk, asshole
beginnen — to begin.
begraven — to bury.
bekend — known. Also: familiar, trusted
beker — beaker, cup, chalice (drinking vessel, often but not always without a handle, generally not made of glass). Also: cup (trophy)
bekijken — to look at, to view.
bel — bell. Also: segment of a rattlesnake's rattle
belangrijk — important.
beleven — to experience, live through, go through. Also: to profess, practice openly (notably a faith or conviction), to live, exist in (a time or space), to observe, follow (a command etc.)
beloven — to promise.
bereiken — to arrive at, to reach, to get to. Also: to achieve, to attain
beroemd — famous, renowned.
beschermen — to protect.
best — quite, rather.
bestaan — to exist, to be. Also: to consist, to be made
betekenen — to mean, to signify.
beuren — to lift, to raise. Also: to collect (debt, owed money), to receive, to happen, to occur, to befall
bevel — order, command.
beven — to shake, to shiver, to tremble.
bewaker — guard (person who protects something).
bewegen — to move, to be in motion. Also: to move, to cause to be in motion, to budge, to motivate, to spur, to induce
bezig — busy, occupied. Also: in progress, on
bezoek — visit. Also: visitors, research, investigation, temptation
bezoeken — to visit.
bidden — to pray. Also: to pray to (often with a preposition phrase), to bid, to hover (of birds), to use wings to remain in the same place in mid-air
bieden — to make an offer, to bid.
bier — beer (alcoholic drink brewed from grains or other starch material). Also: a serving of beer, a variety of beer
bij — alongside, with. Also: in order to adjust or improve
bijbel — bible (copy of the Bible). Also: any large book
bijna — almost. Also: soon
bijten — to bite.
bijvoorbeeld — for example.
bijzonder — special. Also: unusual
binnenkomen — to enter, to come inside.
bisschop — bishop (official in Orthodox, Catholic and some Protestant churches overseeing a diocese). Also: bishop, bishop's wine, bishop
blauw — blue. Also: visibly bruised, pertaining to aquatic nature, drunk
bleek — bleach (chemical used for bleaching). Also: the act of bleaching, bleachfield
blijven — to stay, to remain. Also: to stay, to remain, to still be, to keep/keep on, to continue (remain doing/behaving)
bloed — blood. Also: a grave consequence, a high price, a whole body; especially (plural also (archaic) bloeien in this sense) a (poor) person
bloem — flower, bloom of flowering plant. Also: flour of wheat or corn
blond — blond, fair; of light color (usually said about light hair color, but it can also refer to beer). Also: stupid
bloot — bare, naked, nude. Also: unprotected, uncovered, unaided, simple, uncomplicated

boer — a (male) farmer, peasant. Also: a boor, yokel, ruffian, rustic, a merchant (and sometimes producer) of a certain product group, mainly foods, often named after it, e.g. melkboer 'milkman', groenteboer '(male) greengrocer', a jack (playing card)
boodschap — message. Also: errand
boodschapper — messenger.
boom — tree. Also: any solid, pole-shaped, usually wooden object, beam, mast, boom
boord — border, boundary. Also: shore, bank, collar (of a shirt)
boos — mad, angry. Also: upset, frustrated, evil
bord — plate, dish (cutlery). Also: plank, board (as in "blackboard" (see schoolbord) or as in "chessboard" (see schaakbord)), sign (traffic, etc.)
borst — chest, thorax. Also: breast
bos — wood, forest. Also: jungle, tropical rainforest
boven — above. Also: upstairs
branden — to burn, to set aflame, to be alight. Also: to be lit, to burn, to write to a storage medium
breed — broad, wide. Also: large, ample
breken — to break.
brengen — to bring, to carry. Also: to deliver, to supply
brommen — to mutter, to mumble. Also: to grumble, hum, buzz, drone, to brag, to boast, to do time, to be in prison, to drive a moped
brood — bread. Also: a loaf of bread, a similar bakery product or other baked dish, someone's livelihood
brug — bridge (construction). Also: bridge (dental prosthesis), a substantive connection in a discourse allowing one to move from one subject to another
bruid — bride.
bruin — brown.
buigen — to bend. Also: to bow
buik — belly. Also: paunch (referring euphemistically to a protrusive belly), the lowest inner part of a ship's hull, where water accumulates
buurt — neighborhood, part of town. Also: vicinity, proximity
cadeau — present; gift.
collega — colleague.
contact — physical contact, touching. Also: contact (close association), contact (communication, exchange), contact (someone with whom communication has been established)
cursus — an educational course, on its own or as part of an academic or evening school curriculum. Also: the documentation associated with a course, usually compiled by teachers themselves
daarvoor — pronominal adverb form of voor + dat. Also: before that, previously, formerly, for that, therefore
dagboek — diary.
dame — lady. Also: noblewoman, Polite term or title of address for any (adult or adolescent) woman., queen
dankbaar — thankful, grateful. Also: gratifying, rewarding
dapper — brave, courageous.
das — A badger, various species of genera Meles and Taxidea.. Also: Eurasian badger (Meles meles)
deel — part, piece. Also: volume (of a book or album)
deftig — proper, decent. Also: stylish, distinguished, genteel (showing high social class), pompous
dek — a deck. Also: a cover, a surface

deken — a dean, certain priestly offices. Also: presiding over a chapter, as in a cathedral or collegiate church, pastoral chief in a part of a diocese comprising several parishes, a chairman in certain professional corporations, a doyen, eldest, as in the corps diplomatique, some other presiding officers, e.g. of a neighborhood
detective — private detective. Also: work of detective fiction, such as a detective novel, film, or series
deur — door.
dicht — close, closely. Also: closed, tightly, densely
dichtbij — close, nearby.
dief — a thief, one who steals.
diep — deep, down low. Also: profound, intense
dier — animal (any member of the kingdom Animalia).
dik — thick. Also: fat, cool, nice
direct — direct, immediate. Also: direct, blunt, frank
dochter — daughter (female offspring). Also: offshoot, something derivative or subordinate
doden — to kill.
dokter — doctor (physician, medical doctor).
dom — dumb, brainless. Also: stupid, silly, accidental, thoughtless
donker — dark.
dood — dead.
doodgaan — to die.
doodskist — coffin.
doorlopen — to go through, to go from start to end.
doos — box. Also: vagina, twat, stupid female, twat (objectionable woman), prison

draaien — to turn, to turn round. Also: to play (a record, CD, song, etc.), to input into a telephone, to call (a phone number), to run or throw (a party), to roll (a cigarette or joint), to be the point of, to be about
dragen — to carry. Also: to wear (clothes), to transfer
drank — beverage, drink. Also: alcoholic drinks, taken collectively
droevig — sad, melancholy. Also: gloomy
dromen — to dream.

droog — dry (not wet). Also: arid (getting little precipitation), dry, dull, uninspired, dry; humorous in an understated or deadpan way, dry-tasting, not sweet, having a lot of muscle with a low amount of fat, having gone on a cut
droom — dream.
druk — busy, crowded, hectic (of a place). Also: busy, preoccupied (of a person), restless, uneasy, gaudy, messy, restless
duidelijk — clear, precise. Also: obvious, clear
duif — a pigeon, dove, bird of the family Columbidae. Also: a female dove or pigeon, a dove, someone with dovish political views or inclinations
duin — a dune.
duren — to take, last (a certain time).
durven — to dare (do), to not be afraid (to do). Also: to dare (to), to not be afraid to
dus — so, therefore, thus, ergo.
duur — expensive, dear.
duwen — to push.
echt — authentic, true, genuine, real.
eens — once, one time. Also: sometime, at an unspecified time, modal particle which gives a slightly softer tone to requests or imperatives, even
eerder — earlier. Also: before, previously, more, rather
eerlijk — honest. Also: fair, just
eerst — first, at first, before something else. Also: at first, before, earlier, only, not until
eeuw — a century, 100 years. Also: many years, a very long time; far too long, (an) eternity
ei — egg.
eigen — own, private (not shared). Also: characteristic, typical, specific, Used with a possessive determiner to create a reflexive pronoun.
eigenlijk — actual.
eind — end. Also: a (short) length of something, particularly rope, a considerable distance
eindelijk — at last, finally.
emmer — bucket (container).
eng — scary, creepy. Also: narrow, small
engels — a unit of mass equal to one twentieth of an ounce, approximately 1.5 grams. Also: a physical weight used for weighing gold or silver
enig — only, single.
enorm — enormous.
envelop — an envelope, closing paper wrapper as used for mailing.
ernstig — serious.
ervaring — experience (act of experiencing, something that is experienced). Also: experience (skill)
erwtensoep — pea soup.

even — shortly, briefly. Also: for a short period, for a while, for a moment; modal particle indicating that the speaker expects that something will require little time or effort., just as, to the same degree (used with an adjective), quite, rather
ezel — donkey, ass (Equus asinus or Equus asinus asinus). Also: fool, idiot, easel
feest — party. Also: feast, celebration
film — a film, thin layer or membrane; especially the physical medium film. Also: a film production, movie, the movie sector, cinema
flink — large, considerable. Also: stalwart, brave, sturdy
fluisteren — to whisper.
galg — gallows, wooden framework on which persons are put to death by hanging. Also: the death sentence, notably by hanging, a stake or pole to hang/suspend objects on
gang — passageway, alley. Also: gait, walk (person's manner of walking or stepping), hallway, corridor, course (meal)
gast — guest. Also: knave, worker, apprentice, delivery boy, dude, guy
gat — gap, hole. Also: godforsaken place, hamlet, port
gauw — quickly. Also: soon
gebaar — gesture (act of gesticulating). Also: gesture (act or token of appreciation or lack thereof), movement, motion, appearance, uproar, tumult
gebeuren — to happen.
geboren — born.
gebruiken — to use. Also: to consume, to eat or drink (food or drink), to consume (resources or goods)
geest — ghost, spirit, immaterial being. Also: mind, mental consciousness, mindset, mentality, life force, vital energy, spirit, gas, distillate, essence
geheim — secret.
gek — crazy, mad. Also: ludicrous, farcical, silly, playful
geleden — ago.
geliefd — beloved, object of love. Also: popular, widely liked
gelijk — equal, like, alike. Also: equal, equivalent
geloven — to believe, to hold a belief. Also: to believe, to be convinced by
geluid — sound.
geluk — luck, good luck. Also: happiness
gelukken — synonym of lukken.
gelukkig — happy; generally in life. For momentarily, see blij.. Also: pleased, satisfied, fulfilled, lucky
gemeen — mean, hurtful. Also: intense, extreme, common, shared, communal
gemiddeld — average.
genieten — to enjoy oneself, to have a pleasant experience. Also: to enjoy, to enjoy, to use or benefit from
genoeg — enough, to enough of a degree, with sufficient frequency.
gerecht — justice; the judiciary. Also: court of law, a meal, or a dish served alone or part of a meal as a course
gesprek — talk, verbal conversation.
gevaarlijk — dangerous.
geval — case, situation, instance. Also: thing
gevangenis — a jail, a prison; especially an institution where convicts are incarcerated, but sometimes used more generally.
gevecht — fight, combat, battle, struggle.
gevoel — feeling, emotion.
geweldig — tremendous, enormous. Also: awesome, wonderful
gewoon — regular, ordinary, usual. Also: normal, common, wont, having a certain habit
gezellig — having an inviting and friendly ambiance; cozy, nice, pleasant, sociable, convivial. Also: inclined to seek the company of others
gezicht — seeing, the ability to observe visually, vision, sight. Also: face, front-side of a head, sight, visual impression, view, vision, vision, experience of an appearance, figurehead
gezin — immediate family or nuclear family. Also: entourage, retinue
gezond — healthy, being in good health. Also: healthy, beneficial for health
gids — guide (person guiding others). Also: guide (text providing guidance)
gier — vulture. Also: monster, notably hellspawn, human vulture, rapacious or predatory person, consuming desire
gillen — to yell, scream.
gisteren — yesterday.
glijden — to slide. Also: to lapse
glimlachen — to smile.
goedkoop — cheap, inexpensive, affordable. Also: cheap (of inferior quality or little value), cheap, being uncharitable or simplistic
gooien — to throw.
gordel — belt, a (typically leather) band worn around the waist. Also: belt, roughly belt-shaped area, seat belt
gordijn — curtain (piece of cloth covering a window). Also: net curtain
goud — gold. Also: or, gold
gouden — golden, made of gold. Also: precious (as gold), of great (monetary or other) value
goudstuk — gold coin.
graaf — earl, count.
gracht — canal (in a city, with houses on each side). Also: ditch, trench (in the countryside, referring to both those that contain water and those that are dry)
graf — grave.
grafkelder — a crypt, a burial vault.
grap — joke. Also: an adverse event or unnecessary item that incurs a considerable cost
gras — grass.
graven — to dig physically. Also: to search for information
griep — influenza, flu.
grijnzen — to grin.
grijpen — to grab.
grijs — grey, grey-colored. Also: old, weathered, tarnished, affected by wear and tear, partially illegal
groen — green. Also: pertaining to terrestrial nature, green, relating to green parties, green politics or sustainability in general
groente — vegetables. Also: a (type of) vegetable
groep — group. Also: circuit in an electrical installation, protected by a circuit breaker
groet — greeting.
grond — ground, dirt, soil. Also: floor, ground, ground, land, territory
grootvader — grandfather.

gulden — the guilder (or florin, abbreviated fl.), the Dutch national currency before the introduction of the Euro in 2002. Also: the Dutch East Indies guilder, the currency of colonial Indonesia prior to its 1945 independence, the Netherlands Antillean guilder (now the Caribbean guilder), the national currency circulated in the former Netherlands Antilles (now used in the Caribbean Netherlands)., name of several historical gold or silver coins, the Surinamese dollar

haan — a cock, a rooster (male chicken). Also: the male of various other species of gallinaceous birds, an object resembling or depicting a cock, the lock or hammer of an older type of firearm, a macho; a man who flaunts his masculinity
haard — a hearth. Also: a fireplace, a place where a disease or disaster begins and spreads from
haas — hare, lagomorph of the genus Lepus. Also: meat of the hare, fillet, rabbit, pacesetter (runner in a distance race who sets the pace)
halen — to fetch, to get. Also: to reach (a goal), to catch, to make it
halfuur — half-hour, half an hour.
hals — the neck. Also: the front side of the neck; throat, one's life, survival
hand — a hand of a human, other simian or other animal with fingers.
hangen — to hang.

hard — hard, strong. Also: strong, not easily devalued, unquestionable, uncontestable, heartless, unsympathetic (of a person), hard, difficult, harsh, heavy, hard, rich in calcium (of water), loud (of sound), fast
harnas — (plate) armor. Also: protection
harpoen — a harpoon, short spearlike weapon with a barbed head thrown at the target, of various sizes and types, e.g. used for whaling. Also: some similar weapons, a figure representing a harpoon
harpoenier — harpooner.
hart — heart, main muscle pumping blood through the body. Also: the center point or zone of an object, image etc., the core or essence of some thing, reasoning etc., compassionate or similar feelings
haven — harbour. Also: port
heer — a lord; master. Also: a gentleman, a cleric, notably a Catholic priest
heerlijk — wonderful (when referring to a sensation), great. Also: delicious, lordly, suzerain (pertaining to a feudal lord), glorious, magnificent
heks — witch (sorceress). Also: witch (term of abuse for a woman)
helemaal — completely. Also: all the way, the whole way, especially, even more so, anyway
helm — helmet, protective headwear. Also: helmet above a shield
hemel — sky, heavens (visible sky). Also: heaven (divine abode; type of afterlife)
herberg — inn, lodging.
herinneren — to remind, prompt. Also: to remember, remind oneself, recall
herkennen — to recognize.
hetzelfde — the same.
hoed — a hat, worn on the head. Also: a cover, protecting something else
hoesten — to cough, push air from the lungs forcefully, as because of a cold. Also: to suffer from a (disease which causes a) cough, to clear one's throat, notably by emulating the above
hof — court, residence of a monarch or other high-placed person. Also: court, entourage of a monarch or other high-placed person, court of law; short form of gerechtshof, court, yard, garden
hollen — to run fast or frantically, run away, flee etc.. Also: to rage; be/get out of control, to (be/ go on) strike
honderd — a count of hundred.
honger — hunger.
hoofd — head. Also: head (of an organisation), chief, boss, a transversal dam or pier at the entrance of a sea harbour, oral sex
hoofdstuk — chapter (in a book, film etc.).
hoog — high.
hopen — to hope for, to wish for.
hout — wood (material). Also: particular kind of wood, piece of wood, stick
huid — skin. Also: hide, the hull of a ship or aircraft
huilen — to cry, to weep. Also: to howl, like a (were)wolf or wind
huisarts — general practitioner, primary care physician.
hulp — help, assistance, rescue. Also: helper, aide, assistant, sidekick (also in the masculine gender)
huren — to rent, hire.

hut — a small wooden shed, hut. Also: a primitive dwelling, a cabin on a boat, a usually simple recreational lodging, pub, or suchlike for scouting, mountaineering, skiing, and so on, a roadhouse, inn or pub, sometimes primitive and/or of ill repute
huwelijk — marriage.
huwelijksreis — honeymoon (wedding trip).
idee — idea.
ijs — ice (frozen liquid, notably water). Also: ice cream
inderdaad — indeed.
ineens — suddenly.
informatie — information.
inspecteur — inspector.
intussen — meanwhile, in the meantime.
jacht — hunt, hunting. Also: chase, pursuit
jaloers — jealous, envious (bitterly or enviously competitive).
jammer — unfortunate, sad. Also: too bad, a pity
jas — a coat (outer garment). Also: someone who hasn't been born and raised in Volendam
jawel — an emphatic yes, to indicate strong agreement, or dissent in reply to a negative statement or question. Also: yessir
jazeker — certainly, for sure.
jong — young. Also: new

jongen — a boy, any male child. Also: members of a male 'peers' group, e.g. a military unit, Informal term used to address multiple children of masculine or mixed gender; kids, guys (may be considered insufficiently gender-inclusive), Colloquial term used by men to address other familiar men; mate, dude, bro, a male servant, especially but not exclusively a younger one (using it for older men may be dated and offensive), a male trainee or newcomer in a job
juffrouw — young woman, normally unmarried. Also: female teacher at a primary school or a nursing school
juichen — to shout with joy.
juist — just, at that moment. Also: exactly, conversely, on the other hand; actually; indeed; even (emphasizing a contrast)
jurk — dress.
kaars — candle.
kaart — card (rectangular hard flat object). Also: postcard, playing card, map (of an area), menu (of a restaurant)

kabouter — a (now usually benevolent) tiny folkloric bearded man who traditionally wears a pointy red hat, lives in harmony with nature and resides in mushrooms or trees, similar to a gnome, leprechaun or brownie. Also: a very young female member of the scout movement
kachel — stove (closed heating device).
kalm — calm, tranquil, free of noise and disturbance. Also: calm, composed, cool-headed
kans — chance. Also: opportunity, probability
kant — side, face (of an object). Also: side (as opposed to top or bottom), way, direction
kantoor — office. Also: cabinet

kapitein — captain (person in command of a ship). Also: captain (military officer), Amerindian or Maroon tribal village chief, leader of an ethnic group appointed or recognised by Dutch colonial authorities
kapot — broken, defective, kaput. Also: knackered
kar — a cart, vehicle on wheels without motor. Also: any wheeled vehicle, in particular a car or truck
kast — cupboard, closet, wardrobe.
kasteel — castle, either palatial or fortified. Also: any large, opulent building, in particular a residence, rook, a raised fortification on late mediaeval ships

kat — domestic cat, pussy, Felis silvestris catus. Also: feline, female cat, cat, tortoise (a wheeled gallery offering protection to approaching besiegers; a medieval siege weapon), cavalier (a raised firing platform)
katholiek — Catholic.
keel — throat.
kerel — lad, fellow, bloke.
kerk — church, house of Christian worship. Also: church, confessional religious organization, church, temple, non-Christian house of worship
kerkhof — churchyard, a Christian cemetery. Also: cemetery
kerstfeest — Christmas.
kiezen — to choose, decide. Also: to vote, to elect, choose
kip — a chicken, Gallus gallus domesticus. Also: a female chicken, a hen, synonym of politieagent
kist — a box, chest. Also: a coffin, an aeroplane, a boot or large shoe, especially an army boot
klaar — ready. Also: finished, done (of a task), finished, done (of a person performing a task), clear, pure, not turbid, clear, obvious, evident
klagen — to complain. Also: to sue, to lament, mourn
klant — customer, client. Also: chap, bloke
klap — bang (a sudden percussive noise). Also: blow, hit, strike
klas — a school class. Also: a classroom, travel class (such as first class or economy class)
kleur — a color, colour. Also: a party obedience, as in politics, a suit (of cards)
klimmen — to climb.
klinken — to sound. Also: to clink glasses
klok — clock. Also: watch, wristwatch, bell, protective cloche, something bell-shaped, as some flowers

kloppen — to knock or beat audibly. Also: to defeat, to pulsate, like a heart, to be correct, to be true, to fit, work out, like a calculation; to make sense, to be true, to lead the devoutly professed, unmarried but non-congregational life of a Catholic klop, to , to masturbate
knecht — a servant. Also: an assistant, hand, employee, a flunky; often in diminutive form
knie — a knee. Also: a kneeing, a prod or blow with the knee
knikken — to nod as in to express agreement.
koe — cow; a female of certain mammals, in particular bovines.
koetsier — coach driver, coacher (someone who drives a coach).
koken — to cook, boil. Also: to seethe, boil with anger
konijn — a rabbit, a bunny; any non-hare lagomorph of the family Leporidae.
koning — a king, monarch. Also: a king, top-dog, a king
koningin — queen. Also: female monarchic ruler of a kingdom, consort of a king
kool — a cabbage, plant of genus Brassica. Also: the edible leaves of a Brassica
koopman — a male merchant, merchantman, trader.
koord — a rope, cord (length of twisted strands).

kop — cup (for drinking). Also: head, a (male) human, head of a nail, pin etc., front, lead, e.g. in a race; charge, control, heading (of a text), headline, heads (side of a coin), pegbox (part of a stringed instrument that holds the tuning pegs), one head's height
kort — short.
kosten — to cost. Also: take, require (time, effort, etc.)
kou — cold (low temperature). Also: cold (illness)
koud — cold (temperature). Also: cold (unfriendly), bad, insignificant
kracht — power, force, strength. Also: force
krant — newspaper.

kring — circle; round figure. Also: circle, clique (a community of people with a shared common interest), a circular stain left on a table by a wet glass, the student organization of a university faculty
kruipen — to crawl. Also: to creep
kruis — cross (X-shape). Also: cross (such shape as used in heraldry), cross (execution device with such shape), cross (difficult or tortuous situation), crotch, sharp, heads (side of a coin)
kus — kiss. Also: kiss of peace (Christian greeting), socialist fraternal kiss

vreselijk — terrible.
vriendelijk — friendly, kind.
vrij — free; not imprisoned or enslaved.. Also: free; unconstrained., free; unobstructed, without blockages., free; without obligations or work.
vrolijk — cheerful, merry, gleeful.
vuur — fire. Also: heater, a lighter, the act of firing with firearms, explosives or less commonly other projectile weapons; fire
waar — where, in which place. Also: where, the place that, pronominal adverb form of wat, what, which thing, what, that which, pronominal adverb form of wie; who
waard — an innkeeper, a publican. Also: a landlord, a host, the master of the household; a husband,, a protector, a lord
waarheid — truth.
waarom — why, because of what. Also: pronominal adverb form of om + wat
waarschuwen — to warn, to let someone know, to notify about a threat or consequences.
wachten — to wait.
wagen — a wagon, carriage. Also: a cart, an automobile, car, van, a sled, moving platform on wheels or rails a heavy machine etc. is mounted on, a load filling one of the above vehicles
walvis — whale, mammal of the order Cetacea.

wandelen — to stroll, be on a walk (‘to go for a walk’ = ‘gaan wandelen’). Also: to hike, to walk, to go out (to leave one's abode to go to public places, especially for recreation or entertainment), to take an excursion, to go on a small trip, to go abroad, to travel
wang — cheek.
wanneer — when.
wapen — weapon. Also: coat of arms, a branch or division of the armed forces (e.g., army, navy, cavalry)
warm — warm, hot. Also: 20 °C or more
wassen — to wash, to clean.
weg — away. Also: gone, disappeared, hammered
weggaan — to go away, to leave.
wereld — world.
wijn — wine (an alcoholic beverage produced by a certain fermentation procedure; unless otherwise specified or indicated by context, made from grapes).
wijzen — to point. Also: to point out, indicate, to direct to (by extension, to send to)
wild — game (food; animals hunted for meat). Also: wildlife, wilderness
wilde — savage, uncivilized person. Also: brute
winnen — to win, to triumph, to be victorious (in). Also: to acquire, to extract, to refine, to harvest (from base materials such as ore or crops)

wit — white. Also: legally obtained by having paid the appropriate taxes, having a white skin colour, light-skinned (see usage note), having a relatively light skin colour, clear-lighted, not dark at all
woedend — furious, very angry.

wolk — cloud. Also: visible mass of atmospheric water, visible mass of matter, in the medium air, resembling an atmospheric cloud, visible mass of matter in any medium, resembling an atmospheric cloud, paragon of health (said of newborns)
wond — wound.
wonder — wonder, miracle.
woonkamer — living room.
woord — word.
wrijven — to rub.
zaak — a thing, matter (general term with for a concept other than animate beings). Also: a matter, affair, business, a business, commercial enterprise, store, shop, a court case, law suit
zaal — room, hall. Also: ward in a hospital or similar medical or caregiving institute, Germanic single-room home
zadel — saddle (seat on a horse or a bicycle). Also: saddle (ridge between two hills)

zak — sack or sack-like container, such as a bag, sachet, pouch. Also: a pocket, a scrotum, A contraction of such insults for males as klootzak or rotzak, referring to the scrotum, roughly equivalent to "asshole"., a whore
zakken — to fall, to drop, to sink. Also: to come down, to go down, to fail, to flunk, to calm down
zee — sea. Also: sea (a vast mass, expanse; multitude)
zeker — certain, sure. Also: confident, secure, confirmed
zelf — oneself, referring to the subject of a sentence: myself, yourself, itself, ourselves, themselves etc.. Also: oneself, by oneself, alone
zelfs — even (extreme example).
zes — six (the digit or figure 6). Also: the lowest passing grade, on a scale from 10 (highest grade) to 1 (lowest grade); D− (US)

zetten — to set, to put. Also: to typeset, to make ready, prepare, brew (mostly of hot drinks), to put out (shoewear) overnight, traditionally at the fireplace, now sometimes at the door or radiator, so that Sinterklaas can leave treats or small gifts in it
ziek — sick, ill. Also: excellent
ziekenhuis — a hospital, medical care facility. Also: an infirmary
ziekte — the state of sickness. Also: an illness, disease
zilver — silver. Also: argent, i.e. white

zin — meaning, significance. Also: point, the purpose or objective, which makes something meaningful, sentence, phrase, sense (means of perceiving reality), sense, comprehension, desire, appetite; intention
zingen — to sing. Also: to sing, to blab to the police, to confess under interrogation
zomaar — just because; for no reason, out of the blue.
zon — sun, Sun.
zoon — son.
zorg — care. Also: concern; worry, healthcare, healthcare sector, healthcare services
zorgen — to make sure, to ensure.
zucht — longing, desire. Also: a pathological desire or craving, a disease, sickness
zuchten — to sigh.
zuster — sister (female sibling). Also: sister (fictive female kin member), sister (nun), nurse (woman who provides care for the ill)
zwaaien — to sway, to wave. Also: to brandish
zwaar — heavy, hefty. Also: strong, potent, big, sizeable, difficult, hard, arduous, gruelling, important, significant
zwaard — a sword, heavy blade weapon. Also: a leeboard
zwart — black. Also: dark, black, illegally obtained, without paying due taxes
zwemmen — to swim.
zwijgen — to be silent, to shut one's mouth. Also: to keep silent

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
